howItWorks.intro: This cutting calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Protein = 1.2g per pound bodyweight during a cut
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: How big of a deficit for cutting?
faq.answer: 500 cal/day deficit for 1 lb/week loss. Aggressive cuts (750-1000) lose faster but risk muscle loss. Never go below BMR.

faq.question: How much protein when cutting?
faq.answer: Higher than bulking: 1.1-1.3g per pound bodyweight. Extra protein preserves muscle mass during a caloric deficit.

faq.question: How long should I cut?
faq.answer: Until reaching 10-12% body fat (men) or 18-22% (women). Typical cuts last 8-16 weeks. Take diet breaks every 6-8 weeks if needed.

faq.question: Will I lose muscle?
faq.answer: Some muscle loss is inevitable, but high protein, heavy lifting, and moderate deficit minimize it. Crash dieting causes significant muscle loss.
